conjectures.name: Riemann Hypothesis
conjectures.statement: All non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function \(\zeta(s)\) have real part equal to \(\frac{1}{2}\), i.e., if \(\zeta(s) = 0\) and \(0 < \text{Re}(s) < 1\), then \(\text{Re}(s) = \frac{1}{2}\).

conjectures.name: Twin Prime Conjecture
conjectures.statement: There exist infinitely many primes p such that p + 2 is also prime, i.e., \( \#\{p \text{ prime} : p+2 \text{ prime}\} = \infty \).

conjectures.name: Hardy-Littlewood Prime k-tuples Conjecture
conjectures.statement: For an admissible k-tuple \(\{h_1, h_2, ..., h_k\}\), the number of integers n \le x such that n+h_1, ..., n+h_k are all prime is asymptotically \( \sim \mathfrak{S}(h_1,...,h_k) \int_2^x \frac{dt}{(\log t)^k} \), where \(\mathfrak{S}\) is the singular series.

conjectures.name: Elliott-Halberstam Conjecture
conjectures.statement: For any \( \theta < 1 \), \( \sum_{q \le x^\theta} \max_{(a,q)=1} | \pi(x; q,a) - \frac{\text{li}(x)}{\phi(q)} | \ll x / (\log x)^A \) for all A > 0.

conjectures.name: Generalized Elliott-Halberstam Conjecture
conjectures.statement: Let \(\Lambda(n)\) be the von Mangoldt function. For any \(\theta < 1\), \( \sum_{q \le x^\theta} \max_{(a,q)=1} | \sum_{n \le x, n \equiv a \pmod q} \Lambda(n) - x/\phi(q) | \ll x / (\log x)^A \) for all A > 0.

conjectures.name: Bateman-Horn Conjecture
conjectures.statement: For polynomials \(f_1,...,f_k \in \mathbb{Z}[x]\) with positive leading coefficients, the number of n \le x with all f_i(n) prime is asymptotically \( \sim \frac{C}{\prod_{i=1}^k \deg f_i} \int_2^x \frac{dt}{(\log t)^k} \), where C is the product over primes of local densities.

conjectures.name: Dickson's Conjecture
conjectures.statement: If \(\{a_i n + b_i\}_{i=1}^k\) is an admissible set of linear forms, then there exist infinitely many n such that all \(a_i n + b_i\) are prime.

conjectures.name: Polignac's Conjecture
conjectures.statement: For every even number 2k, there exist infinitely many consecutive primes p_n, p_{n+1} such that p_{n+1} - p_n = 2k.

conjectures.name: Firoozbakht's Conjecture
conjectures.statement: The sequence \( p_n^{1/n} \) is strictly decreasing, where \( p_n \) denotes the \( n \)-th prime. Equivalently, \( p_{n+1} < p_n^{1 + 1/n} \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), which implies \( p_{n+1} - p_n < (\log p_n)^2 - \log p_n \) for \( n > 9 \).

conjectures.name: Granville's Refinement of Cramer's Conjecture
conjectures.statement: There exist infinitely many n such that \( p_{n+1} - p_n \ge (2e^{-\gamma} + o(1)) \log^2 p_n \), where \(\gamma\) is the Euler-Mascheroni constant.

conjectures.name: Hardy-Littlewood Second Conjecture
conjectures.statement: For all \(x, y \ge 2\), \(\pi(x+y) \le \pi(x) + \pi(y)\), where \(\pi\) is the prime-counting function. Equivalently, the density of primes does not increase on shifting an interval.

conjectures.name: Bunyakovsky Conjecture
conjectures.statement: Given \(k \ge 1\) polynomials with positive degrees and integer coefficients, each satisfying the three conditions (irreducibility, positive leading coefficient, and non-fixed-divisibility), assume that for any prime \(p\) there is an \(n\) such that none of the values of the \(k\) polynomials at \(n\) are divisible by \(p\). Given these assumptions, it is conjectured that there are infinitely many positive integers \(n\) such that all values of these \(k\) polynomials at \(x = n\) are prime.

conjectures.name: Chowla's Conjecture on Prime Correlations
conjectures.statement: For distinct non-negative integers \(h_1, \ldots, h_k\), the Liouville function \(\lambda\) satisfies \(\sum_{n \le x} \lambda(n+h_1) \cdots \lambda(n+h_k) = o(x)\). This implies statistical independence of prime factorization parities at distinct shifts.

conjectures.name: Shanks Conjecture on Prime Gaps
conjectures.statement: The maximal prime gap below \(x\) satisfies \(G(x) \sim (\log x)^2\) as \(x \to \infty\). More precisely, \(\limsup_{n \to \infty} \frac{p_{n+1} - p_n}{(\log p_n)^2} = 1\), consistent with a Poisson model for prime spacings.

conjectures.name: Schinzel's Hypothesis H
conjectures.statement: Let \(f_1, \ldots, f_k \in \mathbb{Z}[x]\) be irreducible polynomials with positive leading coefficients. If there is no prime \(p\) dividing \(\prod_{i=1}^k f_i(n)\) for every integer \(n\), then there exist infinitely many \(n \in \mathbb{Z}_{>0}\) such that \(f_1(n), \ldots, f_k(n)\) are simultaneously prime. This subsumes both Dickson's conjecture and the Bunyakovsky conjecture.

conjectures.name: Legendre's Conjecture
conjectures.statement: There is a prime number between \(n^2\) and \((n+1)^2\) for every positive integer \(n\).

conjectures.name: Landau's Fourth Problem (Near-Square Primes)
conjectures.statement: There exist infinitely many primes of the form \(n^2 + 1\). More generally, the polynomial \(f(n) = n^2 + 1\) represents infinitely many primes, with asymptotic count \(\#\{n \le x : n^2+1 \text{ prime}\} \sim C \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\log x}\) for an explicit constant \(C > 0\).

conjectures.name: Parity Problem (Selberg's Parity Barrier)
conjectures.statement: Identified and named by Atle Selberg in 1949. The barrier states: if \(A\) is a set whose elements are all products of an odd number of primes (or are all products of an even number of primes), then, without injecting additional ingredients, sieve theory is unable to provide non-trivial lower bounds on the size of \(A\); also, any upper bounds must be off from the truth by a factor of 2 or more. The canonical example demonstrating sharpness: for the set \(A_1 = \{n \le x : \Omega(n) \text{ even},\, P^-(n) > x^{1/2}\}\) and the primes \(A_2 = \{p \in (x^{1/2}, x]\}\), any Brun- or Selberg-type sieve yields an upper bound of at least \((2+o(1))x/\log x\) for both sets, yet \(|A_1| = 0\) and \(|A_2| \sim x/\log x\). The open conjecture is: find a general mechanism by which sieve methods can distinguish numbers with an even versus odd number of prime factors, thereby breaking the factor-of-2 obstruction unconditionally and in full generality. Resolving this in the context of the twin prime problem would require showing \(\sum_{n \le x} \lambda(n)\lambda(n+2) = o(x)\), which is a special case of Chowla's conjecture and currently out of reach.

related_results.name: Bombieri-Vinogradov Theorem
related_results.statement: For Q \le x^{1/2 - \varepsilon}, \( \sum_{q \le Q} \max_{(a,q)=1} |\pi(x;q,a) - \pi(x)/\phi(q)| \ll x / (\log x)^A \) for any A>0.

related_results.name: Zhang-Maynard-Tao Bounded Gap Theorem
related_results.statement: There exists a constant H such that infinitely many pairs of primes p, p' satisfy 0 < |p - p'| \le H.

related_results.name: Green-Tao Theorem
related_results.statement: The primes contain arithmetic progressions of arbitrary length: for every \( k \geq 1 \), there exist \( a, d \) such that \( a, a+d, \ldots, a+(k-1)d \) are all prime.

related_results.name: Pintz-Goldston-Yildirim (GPY) Theorem
related_results.statement: \( \liminf_{n \to \infty} \frac{p_{n+1} - p_n}{\log p_n} = 0 \), i.e., there exist infinitely many primes with gaps asymptotically smaller than the average gap.

related_results.name: Shao-Teravainen: Bombieri-Vinogradov Theorem for Nilsequences
related_results.statement: Bombieri-Vinogradov type results are established for \(\Lambda(n)\) twisted by a nilsequence \(\psi\) of arbitrary degree, with applications to primes in nil-Bohr sets and the Green-Tao theorem in arithmetic progressions to large moduli.

related_results.name: Dimitrov: Bombieri-Vinogradov-Type Result for Exponential Sums over Piatetski-Shapiro Primes
related_results.statement: A Bombieri-Vinogradov type theorem is established for exponential sums over Piatetski-Shapiro primes \(p = \lfloor n^{1/\gamma} \rfloor\) with \(\frac{865}{886} < \gamma < 1\).

related_results.name: Kerr-Shparlinski-Wu-Xi: Bounds on Bilinear Forms with Kloosterman Sums
related_results.statement: New bounds are proved on bilinear forms with Kloosterman sums \(S(m,n;q) = \sum_{x \in (\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times} e\!\left(\frac{mx + n\bar{x}}{q}\right)\), improving results of Fouvry-Kowalski-Michel and others. Applications include improved bounds for higher moments of averages of Kloosterman sums and fourth moments of Dirichlet L-functions.

related_results.name: Lichtman: Modified Linear Sieve and Twin Prime Upper Bound
related_results.statement: A modified linear sieve is constructed yielding the upper bound \(\pi_2(x) \lesssim 3.299565\,\Sigma(x)\), where \(\Sigma(x) = \frac{2x}{(\log x)^2} \prod_{p > 2} \frac{1-2/p}{(1-1/p)^2}\). Pascadi (2025) subsequently improved the constant to 3.203.

related_results.name: Maynard: Small Gaps Between Primes
related_results.statement: For any \(m \ge 1\), there exist infinitely many n such that at least m of the numbers \(n+1, n+2, ..., n+k\) are prime, where k depends on m. In particular, for m=2, there exist infinitely many pairs of primes with gap at most 600.

related_results.name: Pascadi: Large Sieve Inequalities for Exceptional Maass Forms and Greatest Prime Factor of n^2+1
related_results.statement: For infinitely many \(n \in \mathbb{Z}_{>0}\), the greatest prime factor of \(n^2+1\) is larger than \(n^{1.3}\), improving previous exponents of Hooley (1.1001), Deshouillers-Iwaniec (1.2024), and Merikoski (1.279).

related_results.name: Friedlander-Iwaniec: X^2 + Y^4 Captures Its Primes
related_results.statement: There are infinitely many primes of the form \(a^2+b^4\) with \(a,b \in \mathbb{Z}_{>0}\). The proof introduces the Asymptotic Sieve for Primes and uses the Jacobi symbol of Gaussian integers to break the parity barrier.

related_results.name: Green-Sawhney: Primes of the Form p^2 + nq^2
related_results.statement: For \(n \equiv 0\) or \(n \equiv 4 \pmod{6}\) there are infinitely many primes of the form \(x^2+ny^2\) with \(x,y\) prime. When \(n=4\) this verifies the Gaussian Primes Conjecture of Friedlander-Iwaniec. The proof uses Gowers norm concatenation theorems and the quasipolynomial inverse theorem.
